body.summary: LOCK 016 Review — Well-scoped boundary definition. Correctly blocks runtime. Several consistency gaps with LOCK 015 need addressing before implementation.

review_items.description: boundary_id determinism not specified. LOCK 015 uses deterministic readiness_id (slug + hash). LOCK 016 should state whether boundary_id follows a similar deterministic pattern or if it is static/one-to-one with the spec.
review_items.suggestion: Specify boundary_id as deterministic: e.g., 'boundary-[project_slug]-[boundary_type]-[spec_hash]' or document it as a constant tied to LOCK 016 itself.

review_items.description: adapter_contract is listed as a return field but has no shape defined. This is the most important field — it tells future runtime layers how to plug in. Leaving it unspecified defeats the purpose of defining the boundary.
review_items.suggestion: Define adapter_contract shape explicitly, even if loosely. At minimum: { version, method_signatures: [], lifecycle_hooks: [], expected_providers: [] }. Or mark it as opaque/freeform if intentional for a future lock.

review_items.description: boundary_type has no frozen enum defined. LOCK 015 freezes its status enum. boundary_type should have known values documented.
review_items.suggestion: Define and freeze: e.g., Object.freeze({ RUNTIME: 'RUNTIME', PROVIDER: 'PROVIDER', DISPATCH: 'DISPATCH', PERSISTENCE: 'PERSISTENCE' }) or whatever taxonomy is intended.

review_items.description: status field is listed in the return but has no specified values. Should it reuse LOCK 015's readiness status enum or define its own?
review_items.suggestion: Clarify relationship to LOCK 015 status. Recommend either: (a) reuse LOCK 015 readiness status enum, or (b) define a minimal boundary-specific enum like BLOCKED, GATED, OPEN (documented and frozen).

review_items.description: readiness_level not clearly related to LOCK 015's readiness output. If this is derived from LOCK 015 output, the data flow should be explicit.
review_items.suggestion: Document that readiness_level is passed in via input (from LOCK 015 output) or derived from it. Specify: 'readiness_level = input.readiness_status' or define derivation logic.

review_items.description: No mention of scope enforcement (forbidden imports / drift scan). LOCK 015 explicitly forbids http, fetch, openai, sqlite, express, etc. and requires a drift scan. LOCK 016 inherits the same constraints.
review_items.suggestion: Add an explicit section: 'Scope Enforcement — same forbidden imports as LOCK 015. Drift scan required.' Or reference LOCK 015 addendum section 8.

review_items.description: No required test cases specified. LOCK 015 defines 16 required tests. LOCK 016 needs its own test matrix.
review_items.suggestion: Define minimum test cases: (1) all *_allowed_now flags always false; (2) safety_flags all false; (3) boundary_id deterministic; (4) forbidden_actions populated; (5) required_preconditions lists specific future locks; (6) null/empty input handled safely; (7) output is pure metadata, no side effects.

review_items.description: No mention of data source constraint. LOCK 015 explicitly states manual metadata only, no filesystem scanning. Should LOCK 016 follow the same rule?
review_items.suggestion: Add explicit rule: 'Data source: input metadata only. No disk reads, no package inspection, no network calls.' This ensures the boundary function remains pure.

review_items.description: allowed_inputs has no format specified. Is it a whitelist of field names? A schema? An array of strings?
review_items.suggestion: Specify format: string array of accepted input field names, or JSON Schema reference. Prevent ambiguity when implementing.

review_items.description: All four *_allowed_now flags (runtime, provider, dispatch, persistence) being false correctly blocks execution. This is the core safety property and it's handled well.
review_items.suggestion: No change needed. Confirm in spec that these are hardcoded false, not derived.

review_items.description: safety_flags all false continues the LOCK 015 pattern correctly. Clean separation of human-readable boundaries from machine-readable flags.
blocked_until_future_locks and required_preconditions correctly encode dependency gating. This makes the progression path explicit without enabling premature activation.
review_items.suggestion: No change needed. Consider listing specific lock IDs that would unblock each gate for clarity.
